rulings.Case Title: MARIA PEREZ et al VS. LOLMAN ENTERPRISES, INC., DBA LOLLI'S et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 11, 2011, line 3, DEFENDANT LOLMAN ENTERPRISES, INC. DBA LOLLI'S CASTAGNOLA RESTAURANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is denied. There are triable issues of material fact as to whether plaintiff suffered from adverse employment actions and as to whether Higdon, president of Castagnola, engaged in oppressive, fraudulent or malicious conduct. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS ROBERTS VS. EDUARDO PANIAGUA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Leave To File First Amended Cross Complaint; Declarations Of Elena Asturias, Robert M. Sellars And Lucy Bedolla In Support
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 11, 2011, line 4, DEFENDANTS EDUARDO PANIAGUA, ELENA ASTURIAS, PANIAGUA CONSTRUCTION INC.'S Motion For Order Granting Leave To File First Amended Cross Complaint; Declarations Of Elena Asturias, Robert M. Sellars And Lucy Bedolla In Support IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE IN LIGHT OF CURRENT TRIAL DATE. DEFENDANT/CROSS COMPLAINANTS' DELAY AND LACK OF EXPLANATION FOR THE DELAY INDICATE BAD FAITH. THAT BAD FAITH AND PREJUDICE TO PLAINTIFF IF THE COURT WERE TO GRANT THIS MOTION WARRANTS DENIAL. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT HALL et al VS. FOUNDERS REALTY, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 11, 2011, line 7, DEFENDANT FOUNDERS REALTY, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. DEFENDANT MUST FILE ANSWER WITHIN 10 DAYS. AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION, A WRONGFUL ENDEAVOR UNDER THE SFAC DOES NOT REQUIRE SERVICE OF A NOTICE TO QUIT. AS TO THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF HAS SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGED THAT DEFENDANT ABUSED ITS POSITION OF POWER AND KNEW OF PLAINTIFF'S VULNERABILITY. WHETHER THE CONDUCT ALLEGED IS SUFFICIENTLY OUTRAGEOUS IS A TRIABLE QUESTION OF FACT. AS TO THE 7TH CAUSE OF ACTION, ONE INCIDENT CAN CREATE LIABILITY UNDER THE STATUTE. DEFENDANT MISCITES PODOLSKY. THE COURT WILL NOT CONSIDER THE STANDING ISSUE BECAUSE DEFENDANT FIRST RAISES IT IN ITS REPLY. AS TO THE 10TH CAUSE OF ACTION, DEFENDANT'S NOTICE IS A FACTUAL ISSUE. PLAINTIFF HAS SUFFICIENTLY PLED THIS CAUSE OF ACTION. AS TO THE 12TH CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF SUFFICIENTLY PLEADS FACTS TO MEET THE DEFINITION OF CONSTRUCTIVE EVICTION UNDER CUNNINGHAM. =(302/LMG)
